name: citations
description: The highest-fidelity-risk fixture: cite/citenp inline macros + a bibliography over a shared .bib, verified across the full matrix of a numeric CSL (vancouver) and an author-date CSL (apa), each under appearance and alphabetical ordering. Our output is the citation-js shim rewriting the document (Node-native) then the wasm engine converting; the reference is the external Asciidoctor-PDF gem + asciidoctor-bibtex over the same .bib (reference-src/main.adoc in asciidoctor-bibtex macro syntax, one PDF per variant). Parity is asserted structurally and rigorously: the reference-list entries, their order, and — for numeric styles — the number assigned to each work and used at every in-text citation must match the reference; visible label punctuation ([1] vs 1.) is intentionally not required to match. Author-date bibliographies sort alphabetically regardless of ordering, so appearance and alphabetical coincide in the reference and our output must too (this drove a minimal fix to the shim's reference-list ordering).
